reviews.review_description:
I bought this game after hearing about the new title Knights of Fiona, and I came away very impressed.

I remember hearing about this game a while ago, but it didn't pick it up for some reason, but now I wish I did!

After completing it, I can honestly put it in my top ten games in VR. I thought the story was absolutely amazing. The combat was great as well, and I thought the dungeons were fun. Some of the atmospheres were absolutely amazing. They were some of the most gorgeous I have ever seen in a vr game for real. Lastly, the bosses were some of the best boss battles I have ever seen in VR, and there were plenty of them! 

My only complaints is that the music was too loud in certain points, and you couldn't hear what the NPC was saying at certain points. Mainly, this was because of the victory music. Also, reiterating what others have said, the placing of the items like potions and stuff in the centre of the chest isn't ideal, and the way you switch items also isn't ideal. But these things can easily be improved upon in the next game.

Honestly, this is the JRPG of VR. Everything you can expect from a JRPG is in here and then some. I have no doubt the next game will be amazing, and I can't wait to see it!
When I was envisioning vr I pictured this game where you go on a cool adventure and do awesome stuff this game is genuinely nearing gurren Lagann level of Peakness

reviews.review_title: Bro I’m so so happy I played
reviews.review_description:
I love the combat it really plays in to their strengths as a shooter

But I love the character I can’t count how many times I just standed their laughing and smiling ear to ear
I mean it just might be my personal experience but their really got me to love iris like straight I smile when I see her and they did a great job
I’m just sad I can’t watch or interact with the characters anymore that I can
I really only have one problem is that I felt like they had such a great story but in the combat I find zero enjoyment with the characters saying the same things over and over again when you beat an enemy I think having low slow and meaningful moments and dialogue with the characters are what I think would make this game perfect for me😭😭

10 out of 10🔥🔥

Mechanics are great, visuals impressive.  Haven’t gotten into the story much yet but really enjoying the experience.  

Please add an option for  dialogue to flow without having to constantly press a button in your next game.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the suggestion, I'll let the team know for Knights of Fiona! Enjoy your stay at Grand Amnis!

reviews.review_description:
The story was amazing. The mechanics even better. I also love the customization options you can have for those that explore the area.( i really like the drill hand for some reason). It would be amazing if there was a sequel. Not a milking it for all its worth squel, but a well planned and thought out. I am very curious what happens after the ending.

The one and only thing that I disliked, and by that I mean barely bugged me, was the fact that it shows other mages getting cool melee weapons but we ourselves cannot have.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review and for the high priase!

We are working on a new title called Knights of Fiona. It's not a sequel/doesn't exist in the same universal as RUINSMAGUS, but it's made by the same team. I hope you have a chance to check it out when it releases. 

(I also agree with the weapon feedback, which is something we are going to fix in our new game)

reviews.review_title: Still the one and only VR Jrpg
reviews.review_description: And it was totally awesome
This game is absolutely amazing the visuals the characters and the cutscenes are amazing I hope we see a sequel or more content taking place after the main story.

developer_response.body: Thank you! We recently announced a new game called Knights of Fiona. I hope you check it out, it's not a sequel but a brand new game~

reviews.review_description:
There is something about being able to stand wherever you want whilst the cut scenes are taking place. The subtitles are definitely a hassle as they kind of make it hard to look at the characters in a fully immersive way ( they cover the face when you are looking at them ).
The opening scene where Iris grabs you by the arm feels like you were actually grabbed by the character. And more interactions like this would be welcome as they add to the opportunities to become closer to main characters.

The battle system feels rusty. 
Your grenades and healing items are all in one area of the body and you have to scroll in one direction to access them. If you scroll past something you need, it seems you have to keep going until you find it again.

It might have flowed better in battle if healing potions were located somewhere like your hip rather than everything being bundled on your chest. It makes the game a bit clumpy feeling and means you are constantly having to look down at what you are grabbing instead of focusing on the battle. 
The constant camera movement because of this can cause some motion sickness, which is a shame as mostly it's a great game.

The technology is definitely going somewhere. I knew when I saw Criware in the opening credits that this was going to be my kind of game.

What I really love is that when you are facing a character, their eyes follow you through clever positioning of the static images. You can stand, kneel or sit either side of them and it looks like they are always looking right at you.

With a few tweaks, this developer could likely create some of the best interaction and immersive experiences for VR. 

I found for me the main attraction of this game is not the dungeons, although they are a nice feature. The environments outside of that and the characters interactions are the reason I go back to this game. I'd like to see this area be expanded on.

This game is a hidden gem. Among all the VR games I've played, it is probably the most underrated and overlooked one. The cutscene performances are stunning, and the music is epic. I even went on Steam to buy the soundtrack after finishing the game. I want to say just going through this journey in the virtual reality world is already worth the price. A must play for all action JRPG lovers! Hope to see a sequel to RuinsMagus, with more magic power and more character interactions outside of missions!
Good voice acting, looks nice, fun gameplay, and an interesting story. I would love a sequel. Definitely not a game I'll forget about anytime soon.

developer_response.body: We aren't planning a full sequel, but we do have a new game in the works called Knights of Fiona! It's available for wishlisting now!

reviews.review_description:
I really love this game! It has its quirks and can feel repetitive at times, but it’s so much fun to play, and I really enjoy the story. Also, I absolutely love Iris! Lol.

I’m excited to see what the developers have planned for the future. I’d love to see more anime-styled JRPGs like this. This game was a blast, and I can’t wait to see what’s next!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words, I'm glad to hear you loved your time in Grand Amnis. We are hard at work with our next title, more information in 2025!

reviews.review_description: Problem is I'm a broke a$$ white boy and I can only afford the free trial of this and I didn't get long enough I would like my money back or the game you pick...

UPDATE 2: I give up. I have still been unable to get through this game in large part because the geniudes who developed it refuse to add in Cloud Backup support. So having had to factory reset my Quest 3 very recently, I lost hours upon hours of hard and very repetitive work (for the 3rd time!). I also seen how the developers still never added auto mode for the characters to just talk organically without you staying on guard to press A over and over again, and no way to get rid of the HUGE dialogue boxes that STAY IN THE WAY OF THE FU*FFNG CHARACTER THAT'S TALKING!
No updates, no care for their way overpriced game it seems.
I HATE having lost money to a game that has so many sloppy aspects to it. 
So no updates are ever coming, you get what you get here. Which is already $25 over priced due to the lack of care and content. Get your RPG fix eleswhere (this really isn't much AT ALL in terms of RPG mechanics anyway) as well as any other animegesque VR game. This is not worth it.


UPDATE: I just found out that this must be the ONE game I've played that DOES NOT use the Cloud Back-up system...
I had to factory reset my device twice, and when deciding to play this game again and my hours of progress GONE! I was half-way through the overly repetitive game the SECOND time this happened to me... So not sure if it's a Cloud Save issue or just a glitch in the game.
Definitely knocking off a star or two for that due to any device problems, or even getting a new device like the Quest 3, I'll lose ALL data yet again, leading to hours of potentially wasted time (the game is massively repetitive as is WITHOUT literally STARTING ALL OVER).
Due to the game being so massively repetitive in environments, the combat, and there being nothing but dungeon crawling in lifeless copy-pasted short dungeons (which I'll play Dungeons of Eternity for), the town and characters being almost non-existent and unexplorable, and there being no real story to hook and push me through doing this crap ALL OVER because of how saves are stored; the game hasn't been touched in months.
The developers seem to have thrown it wayside, and the actual content for much more than a tech demo is hardly there...
It's disappointing since it teased a world, characters, and RPG-esquegameplay that captivated for a week, but lost me when I realized it showed it's ENTIREhand in the first 30 minutes...
Definitely $25 over-priced as is. I promise..

ORIGINAL REVIEW: Love the light-hearted anime feel of the game, and the combat is nice and mostly fun  (though a lot more true RPG elements would've went a long way). The repetitive design of the Ruins and creatures hasn't worn thin yet in the short half hour bursts I play it in a day.
The music is also a joy to hear and takes me back to my years of playing games like tge Final Fantasy series and Chrono Cross (amongst a few anime series too).
Things that annoy me a bit are the text boxes. I have VERY limited vision/ zero peripheral vision, so I can only see half the text in the boxes, and since they move with your head, I've no way of reading it (thank God for English voice overs fir the more important bits I guess).
I'm overall very happy to have come across this fun light VR dungeon crawler with great anime aesthetics.
 Highly recommend.
Not what I was expecting would rather play zenith

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you were disappointed. I hope you check out our future titles though!

reviews.review_description: Can't get the English to work so don't play it

developer_response.body: English can be set on the main menu in the options menu. Please contact us on Discord (link in game) or Twitter (@ruinsmagus_en) if you have any additional questions!

reviews.review_title: Fatal UI flaw for some users
reviews.review_description:
I really wanted to enjoy this game, but in its current state it’s miserable to play. I like the art style, character design, combat, and controls.

Unfortunately some of the UI elements are nearly unusable.  Most people don’t have an issue with it, I’m guessing they aren’t using the glasses spacer and / or they don’t have the IPD set to the widest setting.

Shortly after launch I requested a refund because the dialog boxes were pinned to my face and so low that it was very difficult to read the dialog.  If I tried looking down the box would move down, keeping the text in the blurriest part of the lenses, the very edge.

I purchased the game again because it was on sale and I had read that the dialog boxes were fixed.

They’re only partially fixed. You can now look down and read the text clearly…in the center at least. The edges of the character dialog are slightly blurry but readable. Unfortunately if you turn left or right to get a clear view, the box rotates with your head.

The pause menu where you manage your inventory and spells is nearly impossible to use.  It’s very wide and the left and right panels are right at the edge of the lenses and unreadable. When I turn to see what’s on the panels they move with my head so they’re never readable.

The menu in the shop works exactly like I’d expect. It stays still so you can clearly see it by turning your head left and right. This is the way the menus in most games work.

Pinning a menu to the players head in VR is always awkward and annoying. Pinning a menu that takes up the whole field of view is bad design in VR.

Unless they fix this I’m stuck with an unplayable game in my library and wasted money.

If the issue is ever addressed, I’ll update this review.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to inform us about this issue. The dev team is still investigating if they can address it, and improve the overall accessibility for RUINSMAGUS. I hope we can improve your playing experience in the future.

reviews.review_description: Not sure how to get a refund

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear this. Can you send us additional information about what headset/firmware you are using? If possible, please join our Discord and contact us so we can address this issue.

reviews.review_description: Just doing the tutorial and I could see the written translation was immensely bad. Considering I would 100% rely on the written dialogue because audio is in foreign, I just don’t think I will enjoy the immersion in this VR. I’ll ask for a reimbursement.

developer_response.body: We are planning on updating the translation and English Voice Acting soon. We plan on wrapping up the recording next week. I hope you will revisit RUINSMAGUS in the future.

reviews.review_description: Was excited to try out this game to discover it did not have a left handed mode. Can only block with left cast with right. I'm shocked game creators could completely ignore the needs of 30% of their customers. And no response when I try to reach out. Until I can get a refund or they respond to any numerous attempts to contact them this review needs to be 0/5

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear your experience hasn't been optimal. For a refund, you will have to reach out to Meta themselves as we can't provide individual refunds on our end.

Where have you tried to reach out? We want to make sure we are seeing other comments from fans.

You can reach us directly via our Discord (link in the game on the main menu, or on our website, character-bank.com). Reviews aren't a good place to have a back and fourth conversation so please reach out to us there or on Twitter, @CharacterBankEN.

reviews.review_description:
Personally, it only took two very large nails to close this coffin.

Awful gameplay mechanics:
Targeting and hitboxes for spells and enemies are janky at best. Almost all the spells/abilities you unlock after the initial ones are essentially downgrades or loadout fillers I only used once. The blocking and parrying mechanic was so unreliable that I just ignored it in favor of dodging everything and only used it when literally required to. It's also quite easy to accidentally waste your valuable items while trying to cast a specialized spell, as they both use the same button - with only your hand positioning and an (imo) overly-large interaction zone as the differentiating factor.

Lazy difficulty balance/pacing:
Standard enemy battles are a monotonous snoozefest, while bosses are bullet-hell or damage-saturation chaos that devolves to "how fast can you chug potions?" (Better make sure you didn't accidentally waste any trying to cast a spell!) This problem is exacerbated by the fact that you - get this - can't increase or decrease the difficulty after choosing it at the start. This is absolutely mindboggling to me, given the obvious simplicity of the game design and the fact that this is an outdated gameplay mechanic. (Footnote: useless mission scoring system may be a factor in this.)

I am disappointed because I was actually beginning to enjoy the world and characters they were building, but I'd rather just watch a story recap on YouTube at this point. Better than further subjecting myself to this, just to see the ending. The developers seem caring of their community though, so I hope they have a next project, and I hope it goes better.

~5 hours played

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to write a review and explain your thoughts. It's really helpful for small dev teams like us to have this kind of feedback from the community! 

The spells are going to be rebalanced in the next patch. Hopefully it will encourage players to mix up the spells they use. 

For the hand placement/grabbing items, I'll discuss it with the devs to see if we can do anything to address it.
The same goes for changing difficulty. The mission scoring system will be updated in our next patch as well and will actually be important in the upcoming DLC. We are also updating the Quest Board with a feature (for all players) that will allow you to revisit older missions and try for a higher score.  
I'm glad to hear you were enjoying the story. Hopefully, the upcoming balance patch/update will alleviate some of the issues you had.

Thank you again for taking the time to write your thoughts. We will continue to do our best in our next game! We are taking all the comments we got from RUINSMAGUS and will be applying them to our next project!!

reviews.review_description:
I was so excited to play this game when it was revealed and that excitement was shot down by the repetitive gameplay and bland looking ruins themselves, the enemies animations are a bit stiff and the control like the way you reload the magic gauntlet is a bit off in my experience, there's also a lack of oculus scoreboard achievements which I thought would of been implemented since I feel it adds reply value and more to do.
I suppose this is my fault for expecting to much maybe and listening to vr youtubers who keep saying the same crap about every game for example : the best vr jrpg or this game is amazing or comparing it to zelda which is so far from the truth!
I will give the game this the small hub area is very good graphics that's it. 

If the devs read this then I hope you will kindly take my opinion into consideration and maybe update and add a bit more also adding achievements, but from what I have experienced I would not recommend this game until it has been updated a bit and please do not listen to these ridiculous youtubers who keep lying just to get views!!

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing your feedback with us. I'm sorry to hear that it wasn't everything you expected it to be. We are looking into ways we can improve the user experience with post-launch updates. I'll let the devs know that people are interested in achievements, and see what we can do to support that. Thanks again!

reviews.review_description: Wow. I don't even know where to start. One of the most awful things I've ever seen in the game is how this game Fades to Black every 5 to 15 seconds. Have an interaction, Fade to black. Transition to another part of the map, fade the black. Over and over and over again. The controls are poor, the graphics are extremely simple, there's no English audio, and I just don't care anymore. I'm done. Never going to start this game again.

This game took me 4.5 hours to beat, and has all the potential to be amazing, and delivers in none of it.

They recycle the same 10 dungeon layouts, and only kixnit up by adding graffiti, or darkening the lights.

The RPG system is subpart. You can upgrade your gear 3 times. There are only 3 shield in the game, and you are stuck with the same gauntlet, so the weapon system is very tacted on.

The spells are nice, and varied though.

The sound was fun at first, but you can tell they spent a lot of money on that one "epic" song, because it's basically the only song you hear every 5 minutes.
It's supposed to enhance the scene, and it does, but I got tired of hearing it.

The story had so much potential, but every story arc is wrapped up within 3 dungeon runs, and they are never seen again. No characters play a role in the in game or overall plot of the story. They just hint of cliches.

I hate to say it, but this game suffers from lack of fluff. Normally I hate fluff, but with only 26 chapters, this game needs atleast 80 chapters, more time to flush out characters, more time with the npcs for character development, 5x more dungeon layouts, and and more robust equipment system. Gimme 10 levels of gear to start, and give me more actual gear. The shield gets 3 cosmetics but that's it.

This would allow you to play the dungeons, and the epic music drop would be more impactful.

This game would even have made a good Monster Hunter style game with coop, but considering you have enough enhancement currency to max everything 3x over, and the dungeon runs scores are literally pointless, this game fell flat.


There's not even a NG+.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the suggestions and feedback. We are looking into ways we can enhance the experience with post-launch bug fixes and support.

reviews.review_description:
TLDR:
The game does a lot right and has the potential to be good in a few updates but right now it's a solid meh. It's buggy controls, crashes, and bland nature will leave a bad taste. There are better jrpgs out there.

Controls:
Fix your reload sensitivity. Seriously, one of the biggest downsides of the game is the reload mechanic not working. Especially with only 6/7 shots during a boss fight or when enemies surround you. Controls not working ruins the game. I spent far too much time waving my arm like an crazy person, trying to reload my magic. Seated play is worse as I hit my bed or chair before being low enough to reload. Easy fix, allow me to set an unused button for reloading.

Magic:
Very cool at first. Most spell types don't work as intended. Base spell or multi shot were the only quick spells that felt good. Ice turret, fire bomb, and light explosion were the only skilled spells that felt right. Homing bullets suck and miss most of the time. Most enemies are too fast and/or teleport for anything powerful but slow. Wasted opportunity for amazing gimmick.

Story:
Anime tower of babel like Dan Machi or Final Fantasy. Everyone mentioned the same thing expectation wise. Persona like vibes for social scenes, sign me up. In practice, however, everything falls flat. Most characters are rather boring or super cliche. Plot points are seen from a mile away with no options  or choices. I'm on rails for a crap story. Luckily I can skip but the gameplay isn't great either sooooo... What's the point of playing?

World:
No open world. No procedural generation. Dungeons are mostly retextured versions of the same basic layouts. This would be fine if the quest system had me focusing on different aspects but that isn't a thing. Quest system is a story hook machine only. You can't choose anything other than the main quest. That usually means running through the same dungeon 3-4 times followed by a boss. Then redo with new texture and harder enemies. Wanted a great jrpg, ended up with original Mario/Sonic gameplay and worlds in 2022.

developer_response.body:
We are sorry to hear that your experience was less than stellar. We are looking into adding additional quality of life improvements, and your suggestions are helpful. I'll be sure to pass them on to the development team. 

Can you send us more information about the crashes you have experienced? We want to look into them on our end and fix them. You can contact us via Discord (link in the game) or Twitter (@ruinsmagus_en) with more details. Thank you.

reviews.review_description: If you want to play some game like SAO,you need to wait. This game is really different from SAO.￼

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you were disappointed. Even though RUINSMAGUS isn't like SAO, I hope you can enjoy it for what it tries to do :).

reviews.review_title: This game made me tear a muscle but…
reviews.review_description:
This game made me tear a muscle but it also let me shoot fireballs,parry like link,create ice turrets, throw Molotovs, and LITERALLY EXPLODE PEOPLE BY POINTING YOUR HAND AT THEM, not to mention the dungeon crawling. the only thing I don’t like is that you can’t revisit previous missions, And you don’t get a shooting range the only way to use your new magic is by entering the next mission which can be a little annoying but overall great game this is pretty much the rpg elements of zenith but no grinding to get levels you just need to get good.

SPOILER ALERT!
In the dragon boss fight you can avoid like all of his attacks by sitting right up against the wall then shooting it
Amazing and easy to play , feel like inside an anime/manga story (ideal for anime/manga lovers). I can compare the mechanics are in zelda n64 but with more fun in a VR context.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind review!

reviews.review_description: This is a really REALLY good game and I recommend this for every
Honestly 10/10 best game, great ending, awesome game play, and unique bosses. One grip though… WHAT IS THAT LAST SHIELD SLOT FOR, the one under the starter shield. How do I get it?

developer_response.body: The last shield is only available for those who backed the game on Camp-Fire (Japanese version of KickStarter) and can't be obtained normally in the game. Thanks for enjoying RUINSMAGUS, I hope you look forward to our next projects!

reviews.review_title: Great potential game, but still lacking
reviews.review_description:
This is Japanese fantasy game base on Adventure Guild and Dungeon system.

If you like shooting magic from your hand and Japanese Anime style world, this is a good choice, but not really that great.

The game lack in variety and complexity. The combat itself is fun. However, when you play this kind of game, you’ll expect to be able to explore and seek hidden treasure, secret ,and ester eggs. The dungeon is too simple. While there several items and magic, the choice is too few for my standard. The city look seriously cool at first, but quickly turn to disappoint when you walk around.

I wish developers would add much more stuff into game. Still, its fun to play around, but don’t expect much.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to write a review Krisri! 

We are planning on adding some new free content later this year. I hope you check out RUINSMAGUS again when the update is available!

reviews.review_title: I give it 4 out of 5 for the demo
reviews.review_description: I like the concept and the Japanese RPG style, but to be honest I have to say so far so good for the demo

developer_response.body: I hope you check out the full game sometime, glad you enjoyed the demo!

reviews.review_description:
This story and characters are really fun. Like the rpgs I played as a kid. Dub update really brings it home and levels though spars are getting more diverse. Tips for more content as followed
*Training area. Those targets in the base camp are decorative but I would love to test skills and practice over there.
**Please please please allow 2 charge skills to be equipped at once selecting them like your skill shots. They have such different effects that limiting to one feels detrimental and a waste of gameplay versatility.
*Needs more 'stuff' to do outside dungeon like a city to explore and things to interact with capitalise on the vr.
More stuff in the dungeon such as puzzles and exploration.
*items need to be lowered to belt area as I'm constantly using items while trying to Perry my shield.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we will be sure to keep this in mind while working on our next project!

reviews.review_description: I like this game, it’s been fun to play so far but I have one issue. The text box moving is annoying, I wear glasses and I can’t read the bottom of the text box without moving my head down but the box moves with my head making it hard to read. Please add an option to lock the text box in place so that those with glasses can read everything

developer_response.body: We are looking into options to improve readability. Stay tuned for news on our Discord (link in the game) or our socials for future updates!

reviews.review_description: Love the graphics. Can’t wait until they put more faces on NPCs. Fun game for my kid who loves fantasy and magic. I have fun with it too. If you like subbed anime you’ll like this. I haven’t run into any glitches like the other person spoke of.

This game started as something that was very awe inspiring and relatively good combat gameplay, then turned into a adventure which took me in many different moods and witnessed some of the best experiences I have had in vr.

Honestly if I were to score this in a way it would be hard because as a part some things are better than other, the world and setting and music are some of the best I have ever heard and the experiences of the journey are second to none, the real standouts to me are the music and the way everything is visualised was so well done on quest.

If I had to give any criticism only that the levels can seem rehashed, but with all the characters and added things to vary those up its hard to put it down.


Honestly if you want a real vr experience or want to have that feeling of being In a anime this is it.. do not miss the chance with this and you will not regret it.


Leaving ruins magus left me with such a awe struck and comfy feeling once finished I can only praise this and hope we get more.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind review. I hope we can all revisit the world of RUINSMAGUS again someday soon!

reviews.review_description: I love this game,but unfortunately this game haven't Chinese...🥲
I've played this for days and it never gets old
https://www.meta.com/appreferrals/havocowo/6669173709823400/?utm_source=4 (replace meta with oculus)

THIS GAME WAS AMAZING THE GAMEPLAY, CHARACTERS, AND STORY this game was very enjoyable and fun. The only thing I really wished is that there should be a co-op or multiplayer mode and I wish that they should open world of the ruins. Overall this was amazing

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words!!! We hope to continue delivering unique experiences straight from Japan.

reviews.review_title: Lovable and a great action RPG for VR
reviews.review_description:
Very curated, lovable and fun to play, hope for more replayability tho as of now after the story you are done (even if a long one 8h circa) , i now a dlc is coming with new story and a wave style dungeon, hope will be fun with some unlockables or roguelite style action or something... 

Anyway base game is a great game, with some little more love could be great for years!
